summary: Large fighter-decomp matching PR that moved multiple fighter and camera routines from assembly into C, with the PR body specifically noting that both Ice Climbers were started. It added C sources and headers for Popo/Nana, Kirby, Link, Yoshi, Purin, Seak, Zelda, Luigi, and ftcamera, adjusted fighter/core structs to expose needed fields, renamed many fighter source paths into per-character CamelCase directories, and updated obj_files.mk to build the new C objects while retaining remaining unmatched asm.
what_changed: Removed large matched portions from asm/melee/ft/chara/fticeclimber.s and added new C implementations in src/melee/ft/chara/ftIceClimber/fticeclimber1.c for functions including func_8011ED38, func_8011EE18, func_8011EE60, func_8011EEA8, ftIcelimber_OnLoad, ftIcelimber_OnLoadForNana, and ftIcelimber_OnDeath.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftIceClimber/fticeclimber2_nana.c with C implementations of ftNana_OnLoad and ftNana_OnDeath, while adding asm/melee/ft/chara/fticeclimber2_nana.s for still-unmatched Nana assembly.
Added ftIceClimberAttributes in fticeclimber.h with explicit filler regions and fields such as xC4 and xC8 used by Nana/Popo load and death logic.

Replaced asm/melee/ft/ftcamera.s with src/melee/ft/ftcamera.c in obj_files.mk and added C versions of camera helper/update routines including func_80076018, func_80076064, func_800761C8, func_800762F4, and func_80076320.

Added or expanded C matching files for several fighters: ftLink/ftlink.c, ftYoshi/ftyoshi.c, ftPurin/ftpurin.c, ftKirby/ftkirby.c, ftSeak/ftseak.c, ftZelda/ftzelda.c, and ftLuigi/ftluigi.c, with corresponding asm deletions/reductions in their chara asm files.

Updated src/melee/ft/fighter.h to expose more real layout in ftData, CameraBox, UnkCostumeStruct, and several fighter special-attribute unions/structs including Kirby, Seak, Nana, Yoshi, Purin, and Luigi.
Changed ftlib camera-box access from x50 to x48.z, consistent with the expanded CameraBox layout.
Updated baselib jobj declarations/inlines: HSD_JObjGetMtx-related callsites in asm were renamed to HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr, and jobj.h added HSD_JObjUnkMtx_assert and HSD_JObjCopyMtx helpers.

Updated obj_files.mk to compile the new fighter C objects and reorganized many fighter source paths into per-fighter directories such as ftMario, ftFox, ftLink, ftSamus, ftYoshi, ftPurin, ftLuigi, ftCLink, ftDrMario, and others.
Added a small ftDonkey_OnDeath implementation in ftDonkey/ftdonkey2.c and made minor supporting edits in player, stage, ftlib, baselib, lb, mp, and asm symbol references.
smart_moves: Kept unmatched leftovers in assembly while moving matched functions into C, rather than forcing an entire fighter file to be finished at once.

Used PUSH_ATTRS(fighter, ftIceClimberAttributes) to represent fighter-specific attribute copying instead of hand-writing the raw copy loop from assembly, making Popo/Nana load logic clearer while preserving behavior.
Modeled Nana and Popo special state fields in fighter.h before writing OnDeath logic, enabling direct field assignments such as fighter->sa.nana.x2234, x222C, x2230.bits.b0, x2238, x224C, and x2250.
Split Ice Climbers into fticeclimber1.c and fticeclimber2_nana.c and introduced a shared fticeclimber.h, which gives future work a place for shared Ice Climber attributes and helper prototypes.
Expanded CameraBox only as far as needed for matching ftcamera and ftlib callsites, avoiding a fully invented struct while still replacing opaque filler with useful Vec2/Vec3 fields.
Left explicit TODO comments for uncertain data, such as float externs and unknown Kirby structs, which makes the limits of the match clear for later cleanup.

Used small expression-only statements in ftcamera.c, including comments like 'this line changes everything lol', as a pragmatic matching tactic to influence compiler scheduling/codegen without pretending the statement has semantic meaning.
Renamed an asm-local label lbl_8011F060 into global func_8011F060 and updated address references, improving symbol usability while leaving the still-unmatched routine in assembly.
decomp_lessons: For fighter work, matching load/death and item-helper routines often requires first exposing the fighter's per-character special-attribute layout in fighter.h.
Ice Climber work should separate shared Popo/Nana attribute handling from character-specific load/death behavior; a shared header plus separate C units is a reusable pattern.
When a raw assembly loop copies special attributes from ftData into x2D4_specialAttributes, check whether PUSH_ATTRS with a newly defined attribute struct can express the same codegen.
Camera matching may require correcting downstream struct consumers at the same time; here ftcamera.c, CameraBox in fighter.h, ftlib.c, and stage camera declarations were all coupled.
Do not assume a baselib helper name is correct just because many callsites use it; this PR distinguished HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr from the older HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr name and adjusted many references.
Large fighter PRs can mix pure matching with repository organization. The obj_files.mk changes show that path renames and build ordering need to be kept in sync with source migration.
Uncertain struct fields were named with offset-style placeholders such as x222C, x2230, x2234, xC4, and xC8, which is preferable to semantic names without evidence.
Partial C ports should keep float/data extern TODOs when the corresponding data remains in asm, to avoid hiding the fact that the file is not fully decompiled.
naming_conventions: Per-fighter source directories use CamelCase fighter directory names with lowercase file names, e.g. src/melee/ft/chara/ftIceClimber/fticeclimber1.c, ftKirby/ftkirby.c, ftLink/ftlink.c, ftPurin/ftpurin.c, and ftYoshi/ftyoshi.c.

Character lifecycle functions follow names like ftNana_OnLoad, ftNana_OnDeath, ftDonkey_OnDeath, and ftIcelimber_OnLoad, although the Ice Climber spelling in this PR appears as ftIcelimber rather than ftIceClimber.
Unknown or not-yet-understood fields keep offset-style names such as x222C, x2230, x2234, x2250, x2D4_specialAttributes, and x1974_heldItem.
Temporary/uncertain helper structs use explicit unknown names such as UnkFloat6_Camera and ftIceClimberAttributes, with filler arrays marking unknown ranges.
Previously label-like symbols can be promoted to func_ names when referenced as callable/function-pointer targets, as with lbl_8011F060 becoming func_8011F060.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted assembly functions by removing the matched ranges from chara asm files while adding the corresponding C object before the remaining asm object in obj_files.mk.
Used switch/case on func_8026B320 return values in func_8011ED38 to reproduce the assembly branch ladder for held item state handling.
Preserved codegen-sensitive unused locals and expression statements in C files, including unused arrays and no-op member expressions in ftcamera.c.
Used extern float labels such as lbl_804D9830 and lbl_804D9898 while data sections remained in assembly.
Renamed assembly call targets from HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr to HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr across multiple asm files to align symbol naming with discovered behavior.
Kept byte/bitfield operations expressible through UnkFlagStruct bit access, for example fighter->x2222_flag.bits.b5 and fighter->sa.nana.x2230.bits.b0.
Updated object file paths and source renames in obj_files.mk as part of the matching process, ensuring the build selected C objects for newly matched functions.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs for cleanup of ftIcelimber spelling and whether it was renamed to ftIceClimber.
Search for later replacements of extern float labels lbl_804D9830 and lbl_804D9898 after Ice Climber data was decompiled.
Check future PRs for semantic naming of ftIceClimberAttributes fields x0, xC4, and xC8.
Look for later CameraBox field-name refinements after ftcamera.c matching.
Search for HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr follow-up work to confirm final baselib matrix helper naming.
Find later Kirby PRs that replace struct S_KIRBY1 and lbl_803C9FC8/lbl_80459B88 TODO externs.
